source_paper_title: Improved Online Contention Resolution for Matchings and Applications to the Gig Economy
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Pollner_2024, title={Improved Online Contention Resolution for Matchings and Applications to the Gig Economy}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.1388}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.1388}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Pollner, Tristan and Roghani, Mohammad and Saberi, Amin and Wajc, David}, year={2024}, month=aug, pages={1582–1606} }
context:
Section 5 (Discussion), page 20

Our algorithm, as well as all prior work on (RO-)OCRS for matching constraints, works with the degree-bounded relaxation for matchings, which is integral for bipartite graphs, but not for general ones. A natural question is whether better (RO-)OCRS can be achieved for points in the matching polytope, i.e., points which also satisfy Edmonds’ odd-set constraints [Edm65]. Can better bounds be achieved for this polytope, perhaps via a different algorithmic approach?
title: Improve contention resolution for points in the exact matching polytope setting
background_latex:
Let \(G=(V,E)\) be an (undirected) graph. A (fractional) vector \(x\in[0,1]^E\) assigns a value \(x_e\) to each edge \(e\in E\). The (degree-bounded) matching relaxation is
\[P(G):=\Bigl\{x\in\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}^E: \sum_{e\ni v} x_e\le 1\ \forall v\in V\Bigr\}.
\]
The (integral) matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\subseteq\mathbb{R}^E\) is the convex hull of incidence vectors of matchings in \(G\). Edmonds' characterization gives
\[\mathrm{MP}(G)=\Bigl\{x\in P(G): \sum_{e\subseteq S} x_e \le \frac{|S|-1}{2}\ \forall S\subseteq V\ \text{with }|S|\text{ odd}\Bigr\},
\]
where \(\sum_{e\subseteq S}\) denotes the sum over edges with both endpoints in \(S\) (the odd-set constraints).

A random-order online contention resolution scheme (RO-OCRS) for matchings takes as input a fractional point \(x\) (typically in some polytope such as \(P(G)\) or \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\)). Then edges arrive online in a uniformly random order. Each edge \(e\) is independently declared active with probability \(x_e\). Upon seeing an active edge \(e\), the scheme must irrevocably accept it into the output set \(I\) or reject it, while maintaining that \(I\) is a matching.

A RO-OCRS is called \(c\)-balanced if for every graph \(G\), every feasible input point \(x\), and every edge \(e\in E\), the output matching \(I\) satisfies
\[\Pr[e\in I]\ge c\,x_e.
\]
Here probability is over the scheme’s internal randomness, the random arrival order, and the independent activations.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a constant \(c>0.456\) and a (polynomial-time) RO-OCRS for matchings that is \(c\)-balanced when the input fractional point \(x\) is restricted to lie in the exact matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) (i.e., satisfies both degree constraints and Edmonds' odd-set constraints). More generally, determine the best achievable balance factor
\[c^*:=\sup\{c: \exists\ \text{a (polytime) RO-OCRS that is }c\text{-balanced for all }G\text{ and all }x\in \mathrm{MP}(G)\}.
\]
latex:
Let \(G=(V,E)\) be an (undirected) graph. A (fractional) vector \(x\in[0,1]^E\) assigns a value \(x_e\) to each edge \(e\in E\). The (degree-bounded) matching relaxation is
\[P(G):=\Bigl\{x\in\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}^E: \sum_{e\ni v} x_e\le 1\ \forall v\in V\Bigr\}.

\[\mathrm{MP}(G)=\Bigl\{x\in P(G): \sum_{e\subseteq S} x_e \le \frac{|S|-1}{2}\ \forall S\subseteq V\ \text{with }|S|\text{ odd}\Bigr\},

A random-order online contention resolution scheme (RO-OCRS) for matchings takes as input a fractional point \(x\) (typically in some polytope such as \(P(G)\) or \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\)). Determine whether there exists a constant \(c>0.456\) and a (polynomial-time) RO-OCRS for matchings that is \(c\)-balanced when the input fractional point \(x\) is restricted to lie in the exact matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) (i.e., satisfies both degree constraints and Edmonds' odd-set constraints). \]
open_problem_quote: Our algorithm, as well as all prior work on (RO-)OCRS for matching constraints, works with the degree-bounded relaxation for matchings, which is integral for bipartite graphs, but not for general ones. A natural question is whether better (RO-)OCRS can be achieved for points in the matching polytope, i.e., points which also satisfy Edmonds’ odd-set constraints [Edm65]. Can better bounds be achieved for this polytope, perhaps via a different algorithmic approach?
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally search for small graphs and fractional points in the matching polytope that appear hard for candidate RO-OCRS policies, but the space of online policies is large and proving optimal lower bounds is difficult. Still, testing specific algorithms and generating adversarial instances is feasible via simulation and optimization over small graphs.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate RO-OCRS policies (e.g., parametric attenuation/priority rules) can be represented as code with tunable parameters, and their worst-case (or empirically minimized) balancedness can be estimated by Monte Carlo over random orders and by adversarial search over small graphs and fractional inputs. This provides an automatic fitness signal (estimated minimum \(\Pr[e\in I]/x_e\)) enabling iterative improvement or discovery of counterexamples to proposed designs.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks whether the balance factor of random-order online contention resolution for matchings can be improved beyond \(0.456\) when the input fractional point is restricted from the degree relaxation \(P(G)\) to the exact matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\), i.e., additionally satisfying Edmonds’ odd-set constraints. In the source paper, Pollner–Roghani–Saberi–Wajc design a contention-aware attenuation function and analyze contributions from events \(R_0(e)\) and \(R_1(e)\) (zero or one realized neighbor before \(e\)) to obtain \(0.45\) in general graphs and \(0.456\) in bipartite graphs for inputs \(x\in P(G)\). Their discussion explicitly highlights that all known RO-OCRS analyses (including theirs) operate over \(P(G)\), and they pose exploiting \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) as a natural next step.

From the broader CRS literature, Edmonds’ polyhedral description of \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) provides the structural strengthening that could potentially be leveraged online. In the offline setting, Bruggmann–Zenklusen give a \(0.4762\)-balanced monotone CRS for bipartite matchings using a polyhedral viewpoint that explicitly uses the full matching polytope description, demonstrating that odd-set constraints can be algorithmically useful for contention resolution. However, this does not translate directly to the random-order online edge-arrival model: online schemes must make irrevocable accept/reject decisions without knowing future activations, and existing attenuation-based RO-OCRS analyses are largely local (neighborhood-based) and do not obviously exploit global odd-set constraints.

At present, there is no cited work resolving whether \(c^*>0.456\) is achievable for RO-OCRS when \(x\in\mathrm{MP}(G)\). The most promising direction suggested by the source paper is to develop an online rounding/attenuation strategy whose analysis can incorporate odd-set constraints to rule out the tight local obstructions (e.g., the 3-edge path example that makes \(\tfrac12(1-e^{-2})\) tight under certain attenuations). Another plausible avenue is to adapt polyhedral/dual-fitting ideas from offline CRS over \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) to the random-order online model, potentially via maintaining a fractional dual certificate for violated odd sets as edges arrive. Establishing either an improved lower bound (a scheme with \(c>0.456\)) or a matching upper bound (showing \(c^*\le 0.456\) even over \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\)) remains open.

sources.title: Maximum matching and a polyhedron with {0,1}-vertices
sources.theorem_or_result: Characterizes the exact matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) by adding odd-set constraints \(\sum_{e\subseteq S} x_e \le (|S|-1)/2\) for all odd \(S\subseteq V\).
sources.connection: Defines the stronger feasible region \(x\in\mathrm{MP}(G)\) that the open problem asks to exploit for improving the RO-OCRS balance factor beyond \(0.456\).

sources.title: Submodular function maximization via the multilinear relaxation and contention resolution schemes
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces contention resolution schemes (CRS) and shows (non-constructively) that a correlation gap lower bound implies existence of a \(c\)-balanced CRS via LP duality.
sources.connection: Provides the foundational CRS framework and the correlation-gap/CRS equivalence that underlies the RO-OCRS objective \(\Pr[e\in I]\ge c x_e\).

sources.title: Online contention resolution schemes
sources.theorem_or_result: Defines online CRS (OCRS) and develops general techniques for online contention resolution under matroid-type constraints.
sources.connection: Establishes the online model and benchmarks that RO-OCRS for matchings refine; relevant for understanding what improvements are possible when strengthening the input polytope to \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\).

sources.title: Optimal online contention resolution schemes via ex-ante prophet inequalities
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops optimal OCRS for certain structures (e.g., stars) and connects OCRS design to ex-ante prophet inequalities; yields attenuation-style schemes used as baselines in later matching RO-OCRS work.
sources.connection: Pollner et al. build directly on the attenuation approach and analysis style; understanding these techniques is important when asking whether odd-set constraints can enable better attenuation or different rounding.

sources.title: Understanding the correlation gap for matchings
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies the correlation gap for matchings and provides bounds/techniques that relate independent rounding to achievable matching value.
sources.connection: Gives prior correlation-gap lower bounds for the degree relaxation \(P(G)\); serves as baseline when asking whether restricting to \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) can improve the best possible \(c\).

sources.title: An optimal monotone contention resolution scheme for bipartite matchings via a polyhedral viewpoint
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives a \(0.4762\)-balanced monotone (offline) CRS for bipartite matchings using a polyhedral approach that leverages the full (exponential) matching polytope description.
sources.connection: Shows that exploiting the exact matching polytope (odd-set constraints) can improve CRS guarantees in the offline setting; suggests that restricting inputs to \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) may allow RO-OCRS to beat \(0.456\), but does not provide an online random-order scheme.

sources.title: Improved guarantees for offline stochastic matching via new ordered contention resolution schemes
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops improved ordered/online-style contention resolution for (stochastic) matching, achieving the then-best RO-OCRS-style bound \(\tfrac12(1-e^{-2})\approx 0.4323\) for edge-arrival RO-OCRS baselines and \(0.382\) for patience-constrained stochastic RO-OCRS.
sources.connection: Immediate predecessor to Pollner et al.; establishes the \(0.432\) barrier for attenuation analyses over \(P(G)\) and motivates seeking improvements—potentially by using stronger input structure such as \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\).

sources.title: Improved Online Contention Resolution for Matchings and Applications to the Gig Economy
sources.theorem_or_result: Constructs a polytime random-order online contention resolution scheme for matchings with balance \(0.45\) in general graphs and \(0.456\) in bipartite graphs for inputs \(x\in P(G)\), via a contention-aware attenuation function.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: whether restricting inputs to the exact matching polytope \(\mathrm{MP}(G)\) enables a balance factor strictly larger than \(0.456\).
